claim: First visit, hadn't been there before
quote: No, first time, I actually found out about it by accident.

claim: Went with her 10-year-old niece
quote: I went with my niece, she's ten

claim: Found out about the museum from a friend's stories about the mirror room
quote: My friend sent me a clip in her stories, something about a room with endless mirrors

claim: The museum's website raised doubts about whether it was still current
quote: the website looked pretty old, honestly, I even started doubting the museum was still open

claim: Expected an interactive science center for kids
quote: I thought it would be something like an interactive science center, you know, like for kids, with a bunch of stuff you can touch with your hands

claim: Entry barrier: complicated navigation to the museum
quote: the museum turned out to be kind of out of the way, behind a market, the GPS glitched twice because the entrance is from the courtyard, not from the main street

claim: Barrier: slow ticket counter
quote: Slow, honestly, we stood there for like ten minutes, even though there weren't many people

claim: The mirror room — peak of delight
quote: That was just wow, honestly, even better than in the video, there's also quiet music playing there, and the lighting slowly changes color, we hung around there for like fifteen minutes, taking photos

claim: The laser maze was also enjoyable
quote: Also great, actually, fun, we ran around, jumped over the beams

claim: Friction: no parking
quote: there's no proper parking either, I ended up right by the curb

claim: Friction: one ticket counter and the cashier gets distracted by phone calls
quote: one ticket counter, and the same cashier both sells tickets and answers questions on the phone

claim: Friction: no signs to the restroom
quote: We looked for the restroom, actually, it took a while, there are no signs at all, we had to ask the attendant

claim: Friction: restroom was dirty, no paper
quote: it wasn't very clean, honestly, and there was no paper

claim: Friction: unexpected extra charge for the laser maze
quote: nobody warned us at the entrance that some halls were separately paid, I found out about it already standing in front of the door, from the attendant

claim: Plaques are small and glare, hard to read
quote: I tried, but the font was tiny, and the display glass was glaring from the lamps too, I never managed to finish reading a couple of them

claim: The illusion hall has no explanation of the physics behind the effect
quote: here it was just plaques again, without any real explanation of how it works physically

claim: No commentary from the attendant in the mirror room
quote: there was just a woman sitting in the corner watching to make sure nobody touched the mirrors with their hands, there was no commentary at all

claim: The child's emotion as an indicator of the peak experience
quote: my niece was just screaming with delight, jumping around

claim: Spontaneous interaction with another family in the mirror room
quote: a family with kids in the mirror room, we took photos together, exchanged a couple of jokes, it was fun

claim: Took a lot of photos specifically in the mirror room
quote: A whole lot in the mirror room, then a bit more in the maze

claim: Shared the experience in her stories
quote: Yes, I posted them in my stories, actually, exactly like that friend who started all this

claim: Didn't find a cafe or a coffee machine
quote: I thought there would at least be coffee from a machine, but there was nothing at all, no vending machine, no snack bar

claim: The gift stand didn't take cards, couldn't buy a magnet
quote: a small stand with magnets and postcards near the exit, but it didn't take cards, cash only, and I didn't have small bills

claim: Missed having an audio guide or a tour guide
quote: I would've liked an audio guide or a tour guide at least on some schedule

claim: Summary list of unmet demand
quote: Online ticket purchases were definitely missing, and a proper cafe, and signs to the restroom

claim: Willing to return, but not soon and mainly for the mirror room
quote: Probably yes, but not soon, maybe in six months to a year, and mostly for the mirror room

claim: Will tell acquaintances selectively: the room is a must, the planetarium isn't recommended
quote: I'll say the mirror room is a must-see, it's just gorgeous, but you can skip the rest, especially I wouldn't recommend the planetarium, and I'll warn them that some halls cost extra

claim: Already told a coworker and showed photos
quote: I told a coworker on Monday, showed her photos from the mirror room, she got excited to go too, with her son

claim: eNPS closer to neutral with a positive skew driven by a single element (unstable category)
quote: Overall, yes, because of the mirror room, it was worth it for that alone

claim: What would shift it toward promoter: fix the planetarium and add a cafe
quote: If they fixed the planetarium and had a cafe, I'd come back a lot more willingly

claim: Emotional peak of the arc — the mirror room
quote: That was just wow

claim: Emotional low of the arc — the mini-planetarium at the end of the route
quote: That's where it really fell apart, honestly, the projector was flickering, the picture was jittering, the sound was crackling

claim: Moment of truth — the mirror room outweighed the rest of the visit
quote: It's like a good song with terrible sound at the end, you know, the mirror room is just gorgeous, but toward the finale everything fell apart into disappointment with that planetarium and no food

claim: Structural barrier (museum location) is secondary relative to the operational ones
quote: the museum turned out to be kind of out of the way, behind a market, the GPS glitched twice

claim: The key barrier is operational — malfunctioning planetarium equipment
quote: the projector was flickering, the picture was jittering, the sound was crackling, it felt like the equipment was really old and nobody had fixed it in a long time

claim: Metaphor for the visit — a song with terrible sound at the end
quote: It's like a good song with terrible sound at the end

claim: Main insight: the peak comes at the front of the route, but the visit physically ends on the weakest link
quote: I'll say the mirror room is a must-see, it's just gorgeous, but you can skip the rest, especially I wouldn't recommend the planetarium

claim: The respondent gives no numeric score, the recommendation is split into parts
quote: I'll warn them that some halls cost extra

claim: The respondent herself is unsure whether it was a mistake or a deliberate museum practice
quote: maybe they just forgot to update the information on the website, or maybe they deliberately don't write it so a person already there doesn't turn around and leave, it's hard to say for sure

claim: The moment with the attendant singing along is meaningful to the visitor but unlikely to be noticed by the museum
quote: she quietly sang along to the song playing from the speakers, I'm not even sure if she noticed that I heard it, but for some reason it made me feel so warm, really human

claim: The respondent herself believes the museum is unlikely to track such moments
quote: I doubt it, honestly, it's not part of the program, just a coincidence, I doubt they track or value that in any way
